title: Deep repository analysis and upstream synchronization
description: Analyze repository state, check all branches for unmerged work, evaluate upstream commits from LiteLLM/LLMRouter, identify technical debt and areas needing attention. Document findings for other work streams.
closeReason: Deep repository analysis complete. All TG10 branches confirmed merged, identified 4 unpushed commits, 3 pending dependabot PRs, upstream drift analysis complete (~40 commits behind), exceptional test coverage verified (1.61:1 ratio). Comprehensive spec written covering branch state, technical debt, CI/CD health, and prioritized recommendations. No blocking issues found.

title: Formalize deployment and configuration patterns
description: Create universal deployment configurations for any environment (Docker, K8s, cloud platforms). Formalize persistence options (Redis, Postgres, S3). Create environment-specific config templates. Ensure deployable anywhere.
closeReason: Comprehensive persistence and config pattern analysis complete. Spec written to .overstory/specs/RouteIQ-6ef5.md. Found: 5 Docker scenarios, production K8s chart, all persistence backends implemented (PostgreSQL/Redis/S3/GCS), 50+ env vars, AWS deployment guide. Identified 6 critical gaps: no deployment matrix, scattered env docs, missing GCP/Azure guides, no config generator, no migration guides, no environment configs. Recommended 4-week formalization plan.

title: Model integration and routing enhancement
description: Enhance model integration to support any LLM provider. Improve routing strategies and ML models. Optimize model selection algorithms. Ensure seamless model hookup for all providers.
closeReason: Comprehensive exploration of routing strategies and ML models complete. Documented: 18+ strategies, dual integration approaches (monkey-patch + CustomRoutingStrategyBase), progressive enhancement chain (ML → Centroid → Random), security model (pickle protection, hash verification), performance characteristics (Centroid ~2ms, InferenceKNN ~10-50ms), and provider integration patterns. Spec file: .overstory/specs/RouteIQ-8ab4.md

title: Production hardening and quality improvements
description: Fix jank areas in codebase. Add comprehensive testing. Improve error handling and resilience. Enhance observability and monitoring. Ensure production-grade quality throughout.
closeReason: Test coverage and observability gaps cataloged. Found: 6 untested core modules (~7k LOC), 7 untested plugins (~2265 LOC), routes lack OTel spans. Spec written with P0-P3 recommendations. Notable: strong fixture infrastructure, 3031 tests total but critical security gaps.

title: Documentation and pattern formalization
description: Document all modes and patterns. Create comprehensive deployment guides. Formalize architectural decisions. Update README and CLAUDE.md with latest patterns.
closeReason: Deployment & config patterns documented: 5 Docker modes, Helm chart, 8 config presets explored. Identified 10 doc gaps (decision matrix, ADRs, production checklist). Spec written with recommendations.

title: P0 — CDK Foundation (VPC/EKS Auto Mode/IRSA/ECR/cdk-nag)
description: Stand up deploy/cdk/ RouteIqStack porting VSR substrate constructs: network_construct (VPC+7 SGs+11 endpoints incl BEDROCK_RUNTIME+S3 gw), eks_cluster_construct (L1 CfnCluster Auto Mode + IRSA-via-CfnJson factory + OIDC), ecr_construct (immutable+scan + GHCR pull-through cache for ghcr.io/baladithyab/routeiq), nag_suppressions (AwsSolutionsChecks). Parameterize for RouteIQ single stateless pod + chart IRSA SA annotation (values.yaml:206). One pod role: aps:RemoteWrite + rds-db:connect + elasticache:Connect + AppConfig read + Bedrock invoke. Deliverable: cdk/ tree + CDK unit+snapshot tests (cred-free pytest synth+nag gate). ADRs 0030,0027. ~3-4w. OPERATOR-GATED deploy.
closeReason: P0 CDK Foundation COMPLETE (commits 2dbac45 proposal + 3a90096 build). deploy/cdk/ RouteIqStack: VPC+endpoints, EKS Auto Mode L1 + Pod Identity (not IRSA), GHCR pull-through + governance, cdk-nag, single least-privilege pod role. Cred-free gate VERIFIED: uv run pytest deploy/cdk/tests/ = 36 passed (no AWS creds). Chart seams wired + helm lint passing. All 7 child tasks closed. ADR-0030 amended (full rewrite tracked as RouteIQ-1f1b, low pri). Deploy remains operator-gated.

title: P1 — Externalized State (Aurora Serverless v2 + ElastiCache Serverless Valkey)

Wire chart externalPostgresql/externalRedis (values.yaml:316-338) + ESO ClusterSecretStore + boot-render DATABASE_URL. Deploy Aurora in SEPARATE CI stage (~30min rollback). ADRs 0028,0029. ~2-3w.
closeReason: P1 COMPLETE (commit 7c57eba). RouteIqStateStack: Aurora PG Serverless v2 (VER_16_13, IAM auth, KMS, 30d rotation, schema-bootstrap Lambda) + ElastiCache Serverless Valkey (IAM user group, KMS, TLS) as a SEPARATE stack. Cred-free gate VERIFIED: 180 passed (all 3 stacks). Chart wired + helm lint green. 4 follow-up seeds filed (2 high/P1: bootstrap-SG fix + IAM-token runtime half; 2 low). Note: live IAM-path deploy needs the 2 high seeds + the runtime app code.

title: P2 — Config + Observability + Data Lake (AppConfig + AMP/AMG/CW + Firehose)
description: Port config_state_construct (AppConfig App/Env/Profile/Strategy/Version/Deployment + LAMBDA validator + S3 + SNS) + observability_construct (AMP CfnWorkspace + AMG flag-gated + CW dashboard + 9 alarms + 3 metric filters + 2 log groups w/PII DataProtectionPolicy) + data_lake_construct (routing_decision CW Logs->Firehose JSON->Parquet->S3->Glue partition-projection->Athena). Wire AppConfig poll adapter + OTel gen_ai.* emit + structured routing_decision log line. CW log group MUST be CDK-created (MetricFilter pre-exist). ADRs 0026,0027. ~3w. Overlaps P1.
closeReason: P2 COMPLETE (commit 496c242). RouteIqObservabilityStack: AppConfig (LAMBDA validator + LINEAR deploy), AMP + AMG(gated) + CW metric filters (gen_ai.response.model key) + alarms + SNS, Firehose->Parquet->Glue->Athena data-lake, + the app-side structured routing_decision log line. title: P0 CDK: port network_construct.py (VPC + SGs + endpoints)
description:
Port deploy/cdk/lib/network_construct.py from vllm-sr-on-aws/cdk/lib/network_construct.py, adapted for RouteIQ's single stateless pod. VPC routeiq:vpc_cidr default 10.40.0.0/16 (distinct from VSR 10.20/10.30), max_azs=2, nat_gateways=1 (keyword-only, default floor), enable_dns_hostnames + enable_dns_support ON. Three subnet tiers: public (PUBLIC), private-app (PRIVATE_WITH_EGRESS — pod ENIs + interface endpoints), private-data (PRIVATE_ISOLATED — reserved for P1). Tag public kubernetes.io/role/elb=1, private kubernetes.io/role/internal-elb=1. […see proposal §refs]

ACCEPTANCE: deploy/cdk/lib/network_construct.py exists exposing self.vpc, self.public_subnets, self.private_app_subnets, self.private_data_subnets, the 3 SGs (alb_sg/pod_sg/vpce_sg), self.interface_endpoints (6 entries incl BedrockRuntime), and self.s3_gateway_endpoint. […see proposal §refs]

Parent epic: RouteIQ-5a85 (P0). Full spec: docs/architecture/aws-rearchitecture/31-p0-cdk-foundation-proposal.md
closeReason: DONE 3a90096: network_construct.py — VPC 10.40/16, 3 subnet tiers, 3 SGs (two-phase), 5 interface endpoints + S3 gw, flow logs. Verified: 6 VPCEndpoint resources synthesized.

title: P0 CDK: port eks_cluster_construct.py with EKS Pod Identity (NOT IRSA)
description:
Port deploy/cdk/lib/eks_cluster_construct.py from vllm-sr-on-aws, but BAKE IN THE POD IDENTITY DECISION: use EKS Pod Identity, NOT IRSA, per the research verdict (research/notes/final_report_eks-automode-cdk-irsa-podidentity-9dc4a2.md, very high confidence; AWS names Pod Identity recommended for Auto Mode; AWS docs state the eks-pod-identity-agent is built into Auto Mode, BUT the production VSR Auto Mode CDK installs eks-pod-identity-agent explicitly via eks.CfnAddon (vllm-sr-on-aws/cdk/lib/eks_cluster_construct.py, comment: 'must be present for Pod Identity associations to resolve') — resolve. […see proposal §refs]

ACCEPTANCE: deploy/cdk/lib/eks_cluster_construct.py emits an AWS::EKS::Cluster with the 3 Auto-Mode blocks enabled (compute/storage/elastic-load-balancing), bootstrap_self_managed_addons=False, authentication_mode=API. The pod_identity_association() helper emits AWS::EKS::PodIdentityAssociation with a static pods.eks.amazonaws.com trust. […see proposal §refs]

Parent epic: RouteIQ-5a85 (P0). Full spec: docs/architecture/aws-rearchitecture/31-p0-cdk-foundation-proposal.md
closeReason: DONE 3a90096: eks_cluster_construct.py — L1 CfnCluster K8s 1.33 + 3 Auto-Mode blocks; EKS Pod Identity (CfnPodIdentityAssociation) NOT IRSA + defensive pod-identity-agent CfnAddon. Verified: 0 OIDCProvider, 0 CfnJson, 2 PodIdentityAssociation, 2 Addon.

title: P0 CDK: port ecr_construct.py (GHCR pull-through cache + RepositoryCreationTemplate governance on the cached repo)
description:
Port deploy/cdk/lib/ecr_construct.py from vllm-sr-on-aws, parameterized DOWN from VSR's 7 repos to RouteIQ's single gateway repo + the GHCR pull-through cache rule. GOVERNANCE TARGET CORRECTION: the immutable-tag + scan-on-push payoff applies to the repo the PTC rule AUTO-CREATES on first pull (<acct>.dkr.ecr.<region>.amazonaws.com/ghcr/baladithyab/routeiq), NOT a standalone ecr.Repository (nobody pulls from a standalone repo, so governance on it is unobserved; ECR's default for an un-templated PTC repo is tag-MUTABLE, no scan). […see proposal §refs]

ACCEPTANCE: deploy/cdk/lib/ecr_construct.py emits one AWS::ECR::PullThroughCacheRule with UpstreamRegistryUrl=ghcr.io, UpstreamRegistry=github-container-registry, EcrRepositoryPrefix=ghcr, and CredentialArn taken from the construct param (never a literal secret); one AWS::ECR::RepositoryCreationTemplate with AppliedFor=['PULL_THROUGH_CACHE'], Prefix='ghcr', ImageTagMutability='IMMUTABLE' (DependsOn the PTC rule); and a registry scan-on-push for ghcr/* via AWS::ECR::RegistryScanningConfiguration (ScanFrequen. […see proposal §refs]

Parent epic: RouteIQ-5a85 (P0). Full spec: docs/architecture/aws-rearchitecture/31-p0-cdk-foundation-proposal.md
closeReason: DONE 3a90096: ecr_construct.py — GHCR CfnPullThroughCacheRule + CfnRepositoryCreationTemplate(IMMUTABLE) + registry SCAN_ON_PUSH; NO standalone repo; credential_arn is a param. Verified via synth.

title: P0 chart: add loadBalancerSourceRanges + Pod Identity SA binding + AWS_REGION + EcrGhcrPrefix image override
description:
Close the chart seams the CDK foundation requires (deploy/charts/routeiq-gateway/). GAP-1 (genuine chart gap): under EKS Auto Mode managed load balancing the NLB CIDR allowlist is honored via spec.loadBalancerSourceRanges, NOT the legacy service.beta.kubernetes.io/aws-load-balancer-internal annotation. Add (1) service.loadBalancerSourceRanges: [] to values.yaml and (2) render spec.loadBalancerSourceRanges in templates/service.yaml when type==LoadBalancer (guard like the existing nodePort block). Without this an Auto-Mode LoadBalancer Service is open to 0.0.0.0/0. […see proposal §refs]

ACCEPTANCE: P0 EDGE DECISION: loadBalancerSourceRanges ships INERT-UNTIL-LoadBalancer and the public edge is explicitly DEFERRED — values.yaml stays service.type: ClusterIP (verified default at deploy/charts/routeiq-gateway/values.yaml:69) and ingress.enabled: false (:82), so the Auto-Mode managed LB does NOT render by default and alb_sg + loadBalancerSourceRanges have no live consumer at P0; the public edge attaches only when an operator later flips service.type to LoadBalancer. […see proposal §refs]

Parent epic: RouteIQ-5a85 (P0). Full spec: docs/architecture/aws-rearchitecture/31-p0-cdk-foundation-proposal.md
closeReason: DONE 3a90096: chart seams — service.yaml loadBalancerSourceRanges (verified inert at ClusterIP, emits allowlist at LoadBalancer via helm template); values.yaml AWS_REGION seam; Pod Identity SA binding (no role-arn annotation). Bonus: fixed pre-existing grafana-dashboard Helm token collision.

title: P0 CDK: cdk-nag evidenced suppressions (nag_suppressions.py)
description:
Build deploy/cdk/lib/nag_suppressions.py copying only the SHAPE of the VSR file (NOT its 2400 lines). apply_nag_suppressions(stack) fans out to _suppress_<construct>(stack) helpers; each calls NagSuppressions.add_resource_suppressions_by_path(stack, '/<stack_id>/<Construct>/<Resource>/Resource', [NagPackSuppression(id, reason)]). Flag-gated families guarded with getattr(stack, 'x', None) because add_*_by_path RAISES on an absent path under cdk-nag>=2.27. […see proposal §refs]

ACCEPTANCE: deploy/cdk/lib/nag_suppressions.py exposes apply_nag_suppressions(stack) + per-construct helpers with getattr guards. Every NagPackSuppression has a non-empty id, an explicit appliesTo, and a reason containing a why-safe clause, a least-privilege/only-valid-form clause, and an Owner: line. After self._suppress_nag(), uv run pytest deploy/cdk/tests/unit/test_cdk_nag.py reports zero surviving AwsSolutions-* errors and zero CdkNagValidationFailure at both the default and maximal flag surface.

Parent epic: RouteIQ-5a85 (P0). Full spec: docs/architecture/aws-rearchitecture/31-p0-cdk-foundation-proposal.md
closeReason: DONE 3a90096: nag_suppressions.py — apply_nag_suppressions + per-construct getattr-guarded helpers, evidenced AwsSolutions reasons. test_cdk_nag green (0 surviving AwsSolutions errors at default + max flags).

Wire into the pre-push hook / CI. […see proposal §refs]

title: ADR-0030: full rewrite/rename beyond the amendment (IRSA->PodID in title; refresh L1-vs-L2 rationale)
description: The 2026-06-14 amendment header flips IRSA->Pod Identity but the filename 0030-eks-auto-mode-irsa-substrate.md and body still say IRSA. Also the amendment notes (acceptedIssue) that aws-cdk-lib/aws-eks-v2 now makes AUTOMODE the default L2 over native L1, so the 'L1 forces this' framing is a CHOICE inherited from VSR, not a hard constraint. When P0 eks construct lands, do a proper ADR rewrite: rename or supersede the file, refresh the L1-vs-L2 rationale (not just IRSA-vs-PodID). Low priority — the amendment header is sufficient for now.

title: P1 forward-notes: STS endpoint returns for rds-db IAM token; ElastiCache fail-open spend window
description: Carry-forward notes from the P0 review for the P1 state wave: (1) STS interface endpoint was dropped from P0 (correct — Pod Identity creds come from the agent, not STS web-identity) but RETURNS at P1 because rds-db IAM-auth token issuance needs it (discover-network:176-177). (2) governance+quota are fail-open on Redis loss, so ElastiCache Serverless scale-to-zero cold starts open a spend/rate window unless *_FAIL_MODE=closed — ride this on the P1 elasticache:Connect grant. (3) the migrate initContainer is P1-gated (deployment.yaml:42-44 gates on externalPostgresql.host), inert at P0. Fold into P1 epic RouteIQ-6078 planning.

title: P2 follow-up: router_error_log_count filter keys on $.level = "error" but NO emitter produces a top-level `level` JSON key — alarm never fir
description:
[high] observability_construct.py:124-125,314-325 builds the `router_error_filter` keyed on filter pattern `$.level = "error"` (and the `router_error_alarm` reads it). But the ONLY structured JSON line the gateway emits is the `routing_decision` line from observability.py `build_routing_decision_record`, which carries NO `level` key (verified by executing the builder: HAS_LEVEL_KEY: False). A repo-wide grep found NO global JSON log formatter / structlog / python-json-logger / dictConfig anywhere in src/litellm_llmrouter — the gateway logs via plain `logging` text messages, so no top-level `$.level` JSON field is ever produced on that log group. Additionally the construct comment claims the value is the 'Python logging level name, lowercased' but Python's default levelname is uppercase `ERROR`, not `error`. Net effect: router_error_log_count sits permanently at default_value=0 and router_error_a

Source: P2 build review (wf_70799be6-17f). Filed to dynamic queue per directive (file, don't fix inline).
closeReason: DONE 51d42ee (P2-hardening wave): resolved=full with a named cred-free test (per-seed audit). See commit + research/p2hard/review-findings.json.

title: P2 follow-up: Entire P2 metric-filter + lake surface is inert end-to-end until a Fluent Bit JSON-promotion config ships (not in repo)
description:
[medium] All P2 filters/subscriptions select on TOP-LEVEL JSON keys ($.event, $.latency_ms, $.["gen_ai.response.model"]). The data_lake_construct docstring (lines 34-47) and the observability_construct docstring both acknowledge that the default Container Insights Fluent Bit forwards container stdout WRAPPED (router JSON as a stringified `log` field + kubernetes.* metadata), so the top-level extraction yields nulls and a `routeiq-routing.conf` [INPUT]+JSON parser is required to promote the keys. I confirmed NO Fluent Bit config exists anywhere in the repo (only unrelated nginx.conf files). So as shipped, the per-model dimensioned filter, the aggregate latency filter, AND the Firehose->lake subscription all see nulls/no-match in a real cluster. This is documented as deferred (seed S2) and is genuinely a chart/manifest concern, not a CDK deliverable — but it means none of the P2 observability value

Source: P2 build review (wf_70799be6-17f). Filed to dynamic queue per directive (file, don't fix inline).
closeReason: DONE 51d42ee (P2-hardening wave): resolved=full with a named cred-free test (per-seed audit). See commit + research/p2hard/review-findings.json.

title: P2 follow-up: Dual `_COLUMNS` / `ROUTING_DECISION_COLUMNS` contract is duplicated across the CDK construct and src with no shared source — l
description:
[low] The 14-key lake schema lives twice: data_lake_construct.py `_COLUMNS` and observability.py `ROUTING_DECISION_COLUMNS`. They cannot import each other (CDK subtree vs gateway package), so they are hand-synced. I verified they are CURRENTLY byte-identical in both names and order, and each test suite asserts its own copy (test_data_lake.py asserts table columns == _COLUMNS; test_routing_decision_log.py asserts the record has all ROUTING_DECISION_COLUMNS). But identity-SerDe extraction means a future edit to one copy that isn't mirrored will silently drop columns to null in Parquet with no cross-cutting test catching it. Consider a contract test (or a generated/checked-in shared JSON) bridging the two.

Source: P2 build review (wf_70799be6-17f). Filed to dynamic queue per directive (file, don't fix inline).

title: P2 follow-up: P0 prep-only RoutingLatencyByModel filter uses a STALE field contract ($.msg / $.routing_latency_ms) that mismatches the shipp
description:
[low] eks_cluster_construct.py:436-439 (flag-gated OFF via _ENABLE_ROUTING_LATENCY_BY_MODEL) authors a prep-only per-model filter keyed on `$.msg = "routing_decision"` with value `$.routing_latency_ms` — the OLD VSR field names. The actual P2 emitter writes `$.event` and `$.latency_ms`, and the P2 observability_construct correctly uses those. The P0 prep filter would match ZERO events if its flag were flipped. It is dead/gated today and P2 ships its own correct filters, so this is not a live bug, but it is a latent trap: anyone enabling the P0 flag gets a silently-broken filter. The P0 prep filter should be deleted (now that P2 owns the real one) or re-keyed to $.event/$.latency_ms.

Source: P2 build review (wf_70799be6-17f). Filed to dynamic queue per directive (file, don't fix inline).
closeReason: DONE 51d42ee (P2-hardening wave): resolved=full with a named cred-free test (per-seed audit). See commit + research/p2hard/review-findings.json.

title: P2 follow-up: amp_remote_write_grant is a documented-but-never-invoked seam — pod role never actually receives aps:RemoteWrite
description:
[low] observability_construct.py:494 defines amp_remote_write_grant(pod_role), but grep across deploy/ and src/ shows it is referenced only in docstrings — never called. By design (documented in the construct docstring and routeiq_observability_stack.py:35-38) the P0 PodRole lives in a separate stack and wiring it here would break the cred-free/props-only boundary, so the AMP remote-write IAM path is intentionally left unwired at P2. CONSEQUENCE: as built, an ADOT sidecar on the pod role cannot remote_write to the AMP workspace until a combined app.py or operator step calls observability.amp_remote_write_grant(stack.pod_role). This is a real cross-stack integration gap (not a security defect — least-privilege is correct when the grant IS applied) and is out-of-scope for the P2-in-isolation deliverable. Recommend filing a follow-up seed (P3) to wire the late-binding grant in a combined-deploy a

Source: P2 build review (wf_70799be6-17f). Filed to dynamic queue per directive (file, don't fix inline).

title: Pre-existing test failures: 3 OIDC SSO-callback tests fail on main
description: 3 tests in tests/unit/test_oidc.py::TestOIDCRouterEndpoints fail on main (test_sso_callback_rejects_invalid_state, test_sso_callback_missing_code, test_sso_callback_idp_error). VERIFIED PRE-EXISTING: they fail at baseline a956a20 with all P2 src changes stashed — NOT caused by the AWS-rearch work. Discovered during P2 merge regression smoke. Likely an authlib/httpx version drift or fixture issue. Investigate + fix; unrelated to the CDK substrate work but blocks a clean unit-test gate.

title: P1 follow-up: Bootstrap Lambda deploy-break - vpce SG blocks Secrets endpoint from store SG
description: [high] Source: P1 build review (wf_bbdba40f-b5b). Schema-bootstrap Lambda needs the Secrets Manager interface endpoint, but P0 vpce_sg only allows 443 from pod_sg, not the bootstrap/store SG. Fix in P0 network_construct: add the store SG to the secrets-endpoint ingress. ACCEPTANCE: bootstrap Lambda reaches Secrets Manager at deploy; cred-free test asserts the ingress rule. BLOCKING for a live P1 deploy.
closeReason: DONE 7fda292: bootstrap-Lambda 443 ingress to Secrets Manager, owned in RouteIqStateStack against the imported P0 vpce_sg (avoids DependencyCycle). Cred-free test asserts the rule; full-app synth has no cycle. CDK 181 passed.

title: P1 follow-up: IAM-token auth runtime half unbuilt - database.py/redis_pool.py still use static creds
description: [high] Source: P1 build review (wf_bbdba40f-b5b). CDK substrate is IAM-auth-only but database.py get_database_url returns DATABASE_URL verbatim and redis_pool.py reads only REDIS_PASSWORD (no SigV4). IAM-path deploy yields creds the gateway cannot auth with. BUILD rds-db:connect splice behind ROUTEIQ_DB_IAM_AUTH; elasticache:Connect SigV4 behind ROUTEIQ_REDIS_IAM_AUTH; chart REDIS_USERNAME. Chart docs softened to intended/not-shipped at P1 merge. ACCEPTANCE: gateway auths via IAM token (mocked-boto test); flags default off. BLOCKING for a live IAM-path P1 deploy.
closeReason: DONE 7fda292: IAM-token runtime half built. database.py asyncpg callable-password (boto3 generate_db_auth_token, 15-min refresh) behind ROUTEIQ_DB_IAM_AUTH; redis_pool.py ElastiCache SigV4 + REDIS_USERNAME behind ROUTEIQ_REDIS_IAM_AUTH; both default OFF. Cred-free mocked tests (test_iam_auth.py), 325 gateway tests pass. Chart docs un-softened. Never logs a token.

title: P1 follow-up: schema-bootstrap handler f-strings runtime DB user into SQL (hardening)
description: [low] Source: P1 build review (wf_bbdba40f-b5b). Bootstrap Lambda f-strings the runtime DB user into DDL. Deploy-time constant, not request-derived - not a live SQLi vector, hardening only. ACCEPTANCE: quoted identifier or allowlist.

title: P1 follow-up: pre-existing chart POSTGRES_PASSWORD ordering bug - back-port decision
description: [low] Source: P1 build review (wf_bbdba40f-b5b). P1 fixed (side effect) the shipped-chart bug where DATABASE_URL emitted before POSTGRES_PASSWORD (literal var = auth failure). Decide if a back-port to released 1.0.0-rc1 is needed. ACCEPTANCE: decision recorded; back-port filed if applicable.

title: P6: Pod role NEVER receives appconfig:GetLatestConfiguration / appconfigdata:StartConfigurationSession from ANY stack -- no grant seam exist
description: [high] found-by: P6 cross-cutting critique (agent aa8e0c6d, snapshot 7c57eba). P0 forward-comment promises this grant (routeiq_stack.py:279-280: 'P2 (ADR-0026): appconfig:GetLatestConfiguration + appconfigdata:StartConfigurationSession (AppConfig ARN)') but never emits it. Unlike P2's aps:RemoteWrite which at least has a late-binding seam (observability_construct.py:494 amp_remote_write_grant), the AppConfig grant has NO method anywhere: `grep -n 'def grant|add_to_principal|appconfig_grant' lib/config_state_construct.py` returns nothing, and RouteIqObservabilityStack takes no foundation/pod_role arg (routeiq_observability_stack.py:78-92). So even after all three stacks deploy and the operator manually wires the AMP grant, the pod that polls AppConfig (the ADR-0026 config path) will get AccessDenied -- there is no IAM path to GetLatestConfiguration. This is the SECOND missing P2 pod-role grant and is strictly worse than the filed AMP one (which has a seam). The file
closeReason: DONE 51d42ee (P2-hardening wave): resolved=full with a named cred-free test (per-seed audit). See commit + research/p2hard/review-findings.json.

title: P6: P2->P0 routing-log-group coupling is NAME-only (no Export/ImportValue, no add_dependency) -> unenforced deploy ordering; P2 MetricFilter
description: [high] found-by: P6 cross-cutting critique (agent aa8e0c6d, snapshot 7c57eba). RouteIqObservabilityStack references the P0 routing log group by NAME via logs.LogGroup.from_log_group_name (routeiq_observability_stack.py:116-121), NOT by foundation reference, so there is NO Fn::ImportValue and NO add_dependency between P2 and P0 (`grep add_dependency lib/routeiq_observability_stack.py app.py` = empty). The construct docstrings themselves state 'A CFN AWS::Logs::MetricFilter requires its target log group to ALREADY EXIST at deploy time' (observability_construct.py:57, routeiq_observability_stack.py:23-24). Because the coupling is a bare string, CloudFormation cannot enforce P0-before-P2: deploying P2 first (or against an account where P0's RoutingLogGroup is the dev DESTROY/teardown-recreated group) fails the MetricFilter + SubscriptionFilter CREATE with no protective dependency. Contrast P1, whose Export/ImportValue DOES enforce P0-first. This P0->P2 ordering hazard
closeReason: DONE 51d42ee (P2-hardening wave): resolved=full with a named cred-free test (per-seed audit). See commit + research/p2hard/review-findings.json.

title: P6: AppConfig validator Lambda dual-resolution => template + behavior DRIFT between cred-free gate (docker absent: no-op accept-all validato
description: [high] found-by: P6 cross-cutting critique (agent aa8e0c6d, snapshot 7c57eba). config_state_construct.py:328 guards on `bundling_available = shutil.which('docker') is not None` (binary present, NOT a healthy daemon). When docker is absent the code falls back to Code.from_inline(_VALIDATOR_INLINE_PLACEHOLDER) which is `def lambda_handler(event, context): return None` -- an ACCEPT-ALL no-op (config_state_construct.py:87-92, 351). The real validator (lambda/appconfig-validator/handler.py: YAML parse + RouteIQ-shape + inline-secret deny) only ships on the docker path (config_state_construct.py:334-347). Consequences: (1) ALL tests + the cred-free CI gate exercise ONLY the inline no-op path (tests/unit/test_observability_stack.py:34-48 forces _VALIDATOR_ASSET_PATH to a missing dir; the snapshot suite has no P2 baseline at all), so the gate is green on a validator that does nothing; (2) a deploy from a docker-equipped host produces a DIFFERENT CFN template (different ass
closeReason: DONE 51d42ee (P2-hardening wave): resolved=full with a named cred-free test (per-seed audit). See commit + research/p2hard/review-findings.json.

title: P6: RouteIqObservabilityStack (the full P2 composition root) has NO snapshot test -> P2 template never pinned against drift
description: [medium] found-by: P6 cross-cutting critique (agent aa8e0c6d, snapshot 7c57eba). tests/snapshot/ collects exactly 2 tests: test_dev_template_snapshot (P0) and test_state_dev_template_snapshot (P1). There is no observability snapshot baseline (__snapshots__/ holds only dev.json + state-dev.json). The P2 stack is unit-tested (test_observability_stack.py) but only via the forced inline-placeholder path, and a snapshot would be non-deterministic anyway because of the docker dual-resolution (prior finding). Net effect: the P2 template (AppConfig + AMP/AMG/CW + Firehose lake + Athena workgroup) has zero byte-stability guard -- a future construct edit can silently change the P2 CFN output with no failing test. The per-wave P2 review could not see this as a gap relative to P0/P1's snapshot discipline. Not in the ~17 filed seeds.
closeReason: DONE 51d42ee (P2-hardening wave): resolved=full with a named cred-free test (per-seed audit). See commit + research/p2hard/review-findings.json.

title: P6: Routing-log-group naming convention is TRIPLICATED as independent string literals across three files (drift risk beyond the filed _COLUM
description: [medium] found-by: P6 cross-cutting critique (agent aa8e0c6d, snapshot 7c57eba). The dedicated routing log group name is hand-built in THREE places that rely on the unstated invariant cluster_name == 'routeiq-<env>' to stay in sync: (1) eks_cluster_construct.py:412 (authoritative producer) `/aws/containerinsights/{self.cluster_name}/routeiq-routing` where cluster_name = f'routeiq-{env_name}' (eks_cluster_construct.py:119); (2) nag_suppressions.py:331-332 hardcodes `/aws/containerinsights/routeiq-{env_name}/routeiq-routing:*` for the P0 Logs-grant IAM5 suppression; (3) routeiq_observability_stack.py:117 derives `/aws/containerinsights/routeiq-{env_name}/routeiq-routing` when no name is passed (the default cred-free path). They MATCH today, but there is no shared constant -- a change to the cluster-naming scheme drifts the P0 IAM grant scope, the P0 nag suppression, and the P2 filter target apart silently. This is DISTINCT from the filed RouteIQ-199e (which is about th
closeReason: DONE 51d42ee (P2-hardening wave): resolved=full with a named cred-free test (per-seed audit). See commit + research/p2hard/review-findings.json.

title: P6: IRSA-vs-Pod-Identity doc drift is NOT confined to ADR-0030 -- it propagates into ADR-0026/0027/0028/0029 body text and cross-links
description: [low] found-by: P6 cross-cutting critique (agent aa8e0c6d, snapshot 7c57eba). The P0 substrate uses EKS Pod Identity (no OIDC provider, no WebIdentityPrincipal; routeiq_stack.py:163-177, network_construct.py:16-18 drops the STS endpoint 'on the Pod Identity path'). But the ADRs still say IRSA: ADR-0027:43 ('The workload IRSA role (ADR-0030) needs aps:RemoteWrite'), ADR-0028:56 ('the workload IRSA role (ADR-0030) is granted') and ADR-0028:104 ('IAM auth + IRSA'), plus every ADR (0026:162, 0027:165, 0028:161, 0029:151) cross-links '0030-eks-auto-mode-irsa-substrate.md / EKS Auto Mode + IRSA Deployment Substrate'. The filed seed RouteIQ-1f1b is scoped narrowly to ADR-0030 (title rename + L1/L2 rationale). The cross-ADR propagation of stale IRSA terminology into 0026-0029 bodies + the shared filename/link is broader than that seed and contradicts the shipped Pod Identity code.

title: P6: Chart has NO seam to consume the P2 AMP/AppConfig CfnOutputs, and emits no AWS_REGION -- a single shared gap across all three planes (Be
description: [medium] found-by: P6 cross-cutting critique (agent aa8e0c6d, snapshot 7c57eba). RouteIqObservabilityStack emits AmpRemoteWriteUrl, AmpWorkspaceId, AppConfigArn, AppConfigProfileArn (routeiq_observability_stack.py:201-241), but the chart has no documented --set seam for any of them: `grep -i 'amp|appconfig|remotewrite|adot|prometheus.*write' deploy/charts/routeiq-gateway/README.md` returns nothing, and there is no ADOT/collector sidecar in templates/. So the P2 remote-write + AppConfig outputs are emitted-but-unconsumable from the chart side (corroborates filed RouteIQ-27b6 / RouteIQ-4333 but extends them to the chart wiring). Separately, the chart emits NO AWS_REGION/AWS_DEFAULT_REGION env in any template (`grep AWS_REGION deploy/charts/routeiq-gateway/templates/` = empty); values.yaml:566-573 documents this only as an operator-must-set extraEnv hint and frames it as Bedrock/AMP. But AWS_REGION is in fact a SHARED prerequisite of P0 (Bedrock), P1 (the rds-db:connect
closeReason: DONE 51d42ee (P2-hardening wave): resolved=full with a named cred-free test (per-seed audit). See commit + research/p2hard/review-findings.json.

title: IAM-token mint signs with region=None when neither iam_region nor AWS_REGION set on serverless ElastiCache/Aurora
IAM-auth serverless region: fail-loud guard when region cannot be resolved
description: RouteIQ-d3a4 incidental: _region_from_rds_host / _region_from_cache_host are best-effort. Serverless ElastiCache uses shortened region tokens (use1) that the host-parse cannot expand to us-east-1, so the IAM-auth path REQUIRES iam_region or AWS_REGION on the serverless path. If neither is set, _mint_elasticache_token/_mint_db_token sign with region=None (botocore error at connect). Consider a fail-loud guard or a deploy-time required value (e.g. validate region is resolvable when ROUTEIQ_*_IAM_AUTH is set). Files: src/litellm_llmrouter/redis_pool.py, src/litellm_llmrouter/database.py.

title: Chart: DRY the duplicated databaseEnv / envVars IAM DATABASE_URL emission
description: RouteIQ-d3a4 incidental: deploy/charts/routeiq-gateway/templates/_helpers.tpl has two near-identical blocks emitting DATABASE_URL + ROUTEIQ_DB_IAM_AUTH (the databaseEnv init-container helper and the envVars main-container helper). A shared sub-template (define) would DRY it and avoid drift between the init-container and the app container on the IAM-auth path. Low priority, cosmetic.

title: P1-fix follow-up: Redis IAM token cached past 15-min in the long-lived singleton (no refresh)
description: [medium] found-by: P1-fix review (wf_9b7d9516). The ElastiCache SigV4 token is minted once at client build and cached in the long-lived redis singleton; it can outlive its 15-min validity (the 30s health-check reconnect may not always rebuild). ACCEPTANCE: token refresh on the singleton (credential-provider callback or rebuild-before-expiry) so a >15-min-old token never auths.

title: P1-fix follow-up: bootstrap 443 ingress reaches shared vpce_sg (5 endpoints), not only Secrets Manager
description: [low] found-by: P1-fix review (wf_9b7d9516). The State-owned 443 ingress targets the shared P0 vpce_sg, which fronts 5 interface endpoints (ECR/ECR_DOCKER/CW-Logs/SecretsManager/Bedrock) — so the bootstrap Lambda's SG can reach all 5, not just Secrets Manager. Least-privilege would scope to a Secrets-Manager-only endpoint SG. ACCEPTANCE: decide (accept the shared-vpce posture as documented, or split a secrets-only endpoint SG).

title: P3 follow-up: trigger doc-40 option-2 — Newton moment-fit (alpha,beta)->(a,b) replacing the a=alpha,b=beta Kumaraswamy shortcut
description: [high] found-by: P3 build review (wf_124f156c). The shipped a=alpha,b=beta shortcut is the DOCUMENTED default (doc 20 §3.1 option 1), but the P3 review is the eval doc 20 §3.1 option 2 anticipated: it EMPIRICALLY showed a reproducible exploit-decision INVERSION (Beta(51,51) mean 0.5 -> Kumaraswamy 0.9155; Beta(40,40) vs Beta(6,4) ranks wrong), i.e. the distortion is in the MEAN not just the tail as the doc optimistically claimed. ACTION: implement doc 20 §3.1 option-2 — a cached 5-iteration Newton moment-fit matching Beta mean alpha/(alpha+beta) + variance to the Kumaraswamy moments, refreshed when alpha+beta crosses log-spaced thresholds (keeps the hot path at 1 uniform draw + quantile). ACCEPTANCE: a test comparing shortcut-argmax vs Beta-Thompson on Beta(40,40) vs Beta(6,4) shows the moment-fit picks the better arm; mean-preservation within tol across the [0,1] range. Also update doc 20 §3.1 to record that the eval triggered the upgrade.

title: P3 follow-up: tests assert on the Beta posterior mean, not the actual Kumaraswamy DRAW the strategy routes on
description: [medium] found-by: P3 build review (wf_124f156c). The strongest P3 tests assert on alpha/(alpha+beta) (the Beta posterior mean), but select_deployment routes on sample_kumaraswamy(a,b) — so the test oracle measures a DIFFERENT quantity than the shipped decision (the fix-verification-oracle-was-green-on-the-bug pattern), which is why the mean-distortion inversion passed green. The corpus backtest also lands in the high-mean region (0.79-0.95) that never exercises the inversion zone near 0.5. ACCEPTANCE: add a test that asserts on the actual draw/argmax behavior in the 0.5 region; bind it to whichever (a,b) mapping ships (shortcut or moment-fit).

title: P3 follow-up: module docstring overstates backend status — Redis/Aurora PosteriorBackends NOT wired; backend/durable/cost_reward_alpha inert
description: [medium] found-by: P3 build review (wf_124f156c). kumaraswamy_thompson.py docstring claims Redis(hot)/Aurora(durable) backends are wired behind flags, but only InMemoryPosteriorBackend exists; register_kumaraswamy_thompson_strategy never reads kts.backend/kts.durable; KumaraswamyThompsonSettings.backend/.durable/.cost_reward_alpha have zero consumers. Either build the RedisPosteriorBackend + AuroraPosteriorBackend (wiring the P1 substrate — the durable multi-worker posterior story) OR soften the docstring + drop the inert config until built. ACCEPTANCE: docstring matches reality; flags either functional or removed.

title: P3 follow-up: AdapterLoaderPlugin entry-point discovery is dormant — not registered into the gateway plugin loader
description: [medium] found-by: P3 build review (wf_124f156c). adapters/loader.py AdapterLoaderPlugin is implemented + unit-tested but never registered into gateway/app.py _load_plugins_before_routes(), so out-of-tree routeiq.routing_adapters entry-point discovery never runs at startup; AdapterFrameworkSettings.entrypoint_discovery/.capability_negotiation are inert. The in-tree KTS strategy + MLOps feedback loop DO work; only 3rd-party out-of-tree adapter discovery is dormant. ACCEPTANCE: register the loader plugin in the gateway plugin manager behind the entrypoint_discovery flag; a test proves an out-of-tree entry-point adapter is discovered.

title: Core routing-registry deadlock: get_routing_pipeline() self-deadlocks on a cold process (non-reentrant _instance_lock re-acquired)
description: [medium] found-by: P3 build review (wf_124f156c). PRE-EXISTING core defect (git diff main...p3 on strategy_registry.py is empty — NOT introduced by P3, surfaced during it). strategy_registry.py:1386 _instance_lock=threading.Lock() (non-reentrant); get_routing_pipeline() acquires it then, when _pipeline_instance is None, calls get_routing_registry() which re-acquires the SAME lock -> self-deadlock, but only on a truly cold process where _registry_instance is also None. The P3 test documented it (test_kumaraswamy_thompson.py:366) and worked around it. ACCEPTANCE: make the lock reentrant (threading.RLock) or release before the nested get_routing_registry call; a cold-process test proves get_routing_pipeline() returns without deadlock.

title: P2-hardening residual: emit_error_log is wired into log_error_with_trace which has ZERO live callers — error filter still cannot fire
description: [high] found-by: P2-hardening correctness-lens review (wf_c820e476); VERIFIED by orchestrator: emit_error_log is called at observability.py:1245 inside log_error_with_trace, but grep shows log_error_with_trace has NO callers in src/ — so the structured error JSON line is never emitted in the live path and RouterErrorFilter still cannot fire. RouteIQ-731c emitted the line but did not WIRE it. ACCEPTANCE: call log_error_with_trace (or emit_error_log directly) from the actual gateway error path(s) — the exception handlers / middleware that currently logger.error — and a test proves a real error produces the JSON line on the routing logger.

title: P2-hardening residual: verify the Fluent-Bit routeiq_router_json parser actually promotes router JSON to record top-level (reviewer dispute)
description: [high-disputed] The P2-hardening RESOLUTION lens marked RouteIQ-27b6/547d full; the CORRECTNESS lens claims the Fluent-Bit promotion config does NOT lift router JSON to the record top level. Orchestrator check: the config DOES have [FILTER] parser Key_Name=log Parser=routeiq_router_json Reserve_Data=On (fluent-bit-config.yaml:75-80), which is the standard promotion idiom — so the dispute is over whether the PARSER REGEX/format actually matches the emitted routing_decision JSON line. ACCEPTANCE: verify (ideally on a live cluster or with a Fluent-Bit local-run fixture) that a real routing_decision stdout line gets its inner keys (event/latency_ms/gen_ai.response.model/level) promoted to top-level so the MetricFilters match; fix the parser if not. This is the one genuinely DEPLOY-TIME-verifiable item (Fluent-Bit behavior can't be fully unit-tested cred-free).

title: P2-hardening residual: AppConfig poll-interval doc claims server NextPollIntervalInSeconds is honored
description: [low] found-by: P2-hardening review. The AppConfig poll adapter doc/claim that the server's NextPollIntervalInSeconds is honored may not match the implementation (fixed interval). ACCEPTANCE: honor the server-returned interval or correct the doc.

Guardrail-policy CRUD durability to Aurora (config.py:1161/1230/1257) — OUT of the P4 roadmap row (roadmap names governance Org/Workspace/Key + usage_policies budgets only); left in-memory+JSON. File if durable guardrail policies are later wanted. Priority P3.

title: P4 review: Key-scoped spend write/read scope-derivation may diverge — key-level budget enforcement can silently fail-open
description: [medium] found-by: P4 build review (wf_ada49e6c). The Aurora/ElastiCache spend WRITE path and the enforcement READ path derive the 'key' scope from different identifiers, so for key-scoped (non-workspace) budgets the counter can be written under one key and read under another, leaving key-level budget/RPM enforcement seeing 0.0 and failing open. READ side governance.py:644/696 uses scope = ctx.workspace_id or ctx.key_id or 'global', where GovernanceContext.key_id is the API key passed to enforce()/resolve_context(). WRITE side router_decision_callback.py _derive_spend_scope() uses metadata.get('user_api_key') or '_key' or '_user' or 'user_api_key_user_id'. Workspace scope (workspace_id on both) and global scope are correct and byte-compatible; only the key fallback diverges (notably the _user / user_api_key_user_id fallbacks resolve to a

title: P4 review: guardrail_policies and prompt state still hydrate JSON-only even when the durable store is enabled
description: [low] found-by: P4 build review (wf_ada49e6c). gateway/app.py:657-658 always calls load_guardrail_policies_state() and load_prompts_state() from JSON regardless of store.enabled — only governance.py and usage_policies.py got Aurora durability. This is faithful to the ROADMAP-P4 row (which names only governance.py + usage_policies.py budgets for Aurora) and ADR-0023/prompt mgmt are out of the P4 durability scope, so it is NOT a fidelity miss. But guardrail_policies.py (1285 LOC, the 14-check governance layer the roadmap itself cites as the in-app layer behind the deferred native Guardrail) and prompt versioning/A-B/rollback state lose durability across pod churn on the multi-replica AWS substrate — the same class of problem P4 just solved for governance rows. Worth a follow-up seed to extend governance_store.py (or a sibling) to back gu

title: P4 review: Spend WRITE scope mismatch makes workspace budgets inert
description: [high] found-by: P4 build review (wf_ada49e6c). writer top-level vs nested _governance_ctx

title: P4 review: Governance budget + rate-limit enforcement is fail-OPEN (P4 wires the counters but the deny path still fails open on Redis/store
description: [medium] found-by: P4 build review (wf_ada49e6c). governance.py:425-471 check_budget()/check_rate_limit() return True (allow) when (a) spend/rpm read returns None — Redis down or not configured — and (b) ANY exception ('Budget check failed (fail-open)'). P4 is the WRITE path that makes these reads non-zero (record_governance_spend + _record_post_response_spend; the comment at governance.py:732 confirms 'Before P4 nothing wrote these keys, so durable budget + RPM enforcement was inert (always saw 0.0 -> fail-open)'). So P4 materially improves enforcement, but the steady-state posture is still: if ElastiCache is unreachable AND the Aurora durable fallback (_get_durable_spend) returns None/errors, an over-budget or over-RPM tenant is ALLOWED through. The requirement asks for fail-closed 'where it matters (governance/quota)'. This is a delibe

title: P4 review: NetworkPolicy egress still allows 0.0.0.0/0 (minus RFC-1918) on 443/80 by default — public egress is not allowlisted
description: [low] found-by: P4 build review (wf_ada49e6c). networkpolicy.yaml:53-66 (unchanged by P4) emits the default allowHttpsExternal rule as ipBlock cidr 0.0.0.0/0 with except RFC-1918, ports 443/80. P4 ADDS a correctly-scoped egress.inVpc rule (template lines 68-82) for Aurora 5432 / ElastiCache 6379 / interface-endpoint 443 pinned to the CDK vpc_cidr 10.40.0.0/16 — that part restricts in-VPC egress properly. But the OUTBOUND-to-internet rule is still wide-open to all public IPs (data-exfil surface: a compromised pod can POST anywhere on 443/80, not just sanctioned LLM provider/S3 endpoints). values.yaml documents that strict default-deny is opt-in via allowHttpsExternal:false + explicit egress.to, and the wide rule is needed for S3 gateway-endpoint + public LLM providers. So the criterion 'NetworkPolicy actually restricts egress (not 0.0.0

title: P4 review: OIDC secret name-alignment is silent-fail (drift -> public-client fallback) — operational footgun, not a code vuln
description: [low] found-by: P4 build review (wf_ada49e6c). By design, if oidc.existingSecretKey and the ESO data[].secretKey ('oidc-client-secret') drift, or oidc.existingSecret != ESO target.name, the chart still renders but ROUTEIQ_OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET is ABSENT and the app silently degrades to OIDC public-client mode (no client authentication to the IdP). This is documented in values.yaml and README ('load-bearing... silent public-client fallback') so it is a known tradeoff, not a defect — but a misconfig downgrades the auth posture without any error. Not P4-introduced as a vuln (the secretKeyRef wiring itself is correct and secret-safe). Recommend a render-time assertion/NOTES.txt warning when oidc.enabled=true but oidc.existingSecret is empty, to surface the public-client fallback loudly.
